videos.title: Collecting and Organising Data | Data Handling for Kids
videos.description: Learn how to collect information and organise it neatly. See how asking questions and recording answers helps us understand things around us, like favourite fruits or colours!
learningObjectives: Learn how to collect data by asking questions

videos.title: Counting and Sorting Objects | Sorting into Groups
videos.description: Learn how to count objects and sort them into groups by colour, shape, or size. Sorting is the first step to organising data!
keyConceptsCovered: Sorting by colour, shape, and size

videos.title: More, Less, Most, Least | Comparison Words for Kids
videos.description: Learn the words we use to compare groups: more, fewer, most, least, equal. These words help us describe and compare data!
learningObjectives: Compare data from two or more groups
Describe which group has more or fewer

videos.title: Tally Marks | How to Count Using Tally Marks
videos.description: Learn how to use tally marks to count and record data quickly. Every 5th mark goes across to make counting easy. Practice tally marks with fun examples!
learningObjectives: Use tally marks to record data
Count tally marks in groups of 5

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Quick introduction to data collection and tally marks for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of data collection and organising

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Start with sorting and comparison review, then data collection

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: You want to find out the favourite game of your classmates. What should you do first?
options: Ask each classmate their favourite game
questions.explanation: To collect data, you first need to ask questions. Ask each classmate their favourite game and write down their answers.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Start with a real classroom survey. Ask students their favourite fruit or colour and record the answers together on the board. This makes data collection tangible.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child collect data at home. Count different types of shoes, sort laundry by colour, or ask family members their favourite food.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Data is just information! When you ask your friends their favourite game and write down the answers, you are collecting data. It is easy and fun!
